title: Why Spaced Repetition
description: Understand why spaced repetition dramatically improves learning, retention, and long-term mastery.

lessons.title: The Forgetting Curve
lessons.summary: Without review, most learning fades quickly.

steps.text: Without review, most learning fades quickly.
Human memory decays rapidly unless information is revisited.
Most learners forget the majority of new information within days.
Spaced repetition interrupts this decay by reviewing material at increasing intervals.

steps.question: What typically happens to new learning without review?
steps.explanation: Without review, most new learning fades quickly.

steps.question: What does spaced repetition interrupt?
steps.explanation: Spaced repetition interrupts memory decay over time.

steps.title: Next Step
steps.text: Come back to this concept after a short delay, then again later, and again tomorrow. Each successful recall strengthens long-term retention.
Active recall means trying to retrieve information from memory rather than simply reviewing it.
The effort of retrieving information strengthens neural connections.
Testing yourself is far more effective than rereading notes.

steps.question: Which activity best represents active recall?
options: Trying to answer a question from memory
steps.explanation: Active recall requires retrieving the information from memory rather than reviewing it passively.

steps.title: Next Step
steps.text: Combining active recall with spaced repetition produces extremely durable learning.
The spacing effect shows that learning is stronger when study sessions are distributed over time.
Each time you successfully recall information after a delay, the memory becomes more stable.
Short repeated reviews spaced across days produce better retention than one long study session.

steps.question: What improves long-term retention the most?
options: Many short reviews spaced over time
steps.explanation: Spacing reviews across time strengthens long-term retention.

steps.title: Course Complete
steps.text: You now understand the key principles behind spaced repetition and active recall. Next, you will experience those ideas in a practical lesson using beginner AI concepts.

lessons.title: What AI Systems Are Made Of
lessons.summary: Learn three core AI ideas while experiencing spaced repetition in action.

steps.text: In this lesson, you will learn three connected AI ideas: model, neural network, and training. You will also see how spaced repetition works by revisiting earlier ideas as new ones are introduced.
A model is a trained system that recognizes patterns in data and produces predictions or responses.

steps.prompt: A trained system that recognizes patterns in data is called a
steps.retryPrompt: ChatGPT is powered by a trained AI .

steps.question: What does a model do?
steps.explanation: A model recognizes patterns in data and produces predictions or responses.

steps.text: A neural network is a type of model made of many connected units that learn patterns from data.
Many modern AI systems, including ChatGPT, use neural network models.

steps.prompt: A type of model made of many connected units that learn patterns is called a
steps.retryPrompt: Many modern AI systems use a

steps.question: What is a neural network?
steps.explanation: A neural network is a type of model.

steps.prompt: A neural network is a type of
steps.retryPrompt: Model is the broader term. A neural network is one type of

steps.text: Training is the process where a model learns patterns by adjusting itself using large amounts of data.
During training, the neural network improves its ability to recognize patterns.

steps.prompt: The process where a model learns patterns from data is called
steps.retryPrompt: Before a model becomes useful, it usually goes through

steps.question: What happens during training?
options: The model learns patterns from data
steps.explanation: During training, the model learns patterns from data.

steps.prompt: A neural network becomes a useful model through
steps.retryPrompt: The process that helps a model learn from data is called

steps.text: Notice what just happened: earlier terms appeared again as new ideas were introduced. That is spaced repetition in action.

steps.title: Next Step
steps.text: In the next lesson, you will learn how people interact with AI systems through prompts, tokens, and context windows.
